title: Handshake vs Waalaxy — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Waalaxy for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and scalability side-by-side to pick the right tool for your outreach.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform purpose-built for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard, with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included in every plan. Pricing is based on the number of sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team collaborates without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Waalaxy is a LinkedIn automation tool designed for simplicity and accessibility. Originally built as a Chrome extension (successor to Prospectin), it's one of the most popular LinkedIn automation tools in Europe with over 100,000 users. Waalaxy offers a free plan with limited actions, and paid plans starting at approximately €19/month (Pro) up to €69/month (Business) per user. Features include automated connection requests, messages, profile visits, email finding, AI message writing (Waami), and 99+ pre-built campaign templates. Waalaxy recently added a cloud component, but its core functionality still relies on the Chrome extension.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — automatically rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — each account is managed individually with no rotation logic

featureComparison.handshake: Fully cloud-based — runs 24/7 without your browser or computer being on
featureComparison.competitor: Chrome extension-based — historically requires browser to be open; cloud component added but extension still required for login and some features

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up over 3 weeks for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Daily quotas and random delays for safety, but no dedicated warmup scheduling feature

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox aggregating conversations from all sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Inbox Waalaxy add-on (€20/mo extra) for LinkedIn conversation management — single account only, not cross-account

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included on every plan, human-like sending patterns, smart rate limiting
featureComparison.competitor: Random delays and daily limits to mimic human behavior; Chrome extension architecture is more detectable by LinkedIn than cloud-based tools

featureComparison.handshake: Clean dashboard designed for teams — moderate learning curve
featureComparison.competitor: Extremely intuitive — designed for non-technical users, 99+ pre-built campaign templates, setup in minutes

featureComparison.handshake: No free plan — 14-day free trial available
featureComparison.competitor: Permanent free plan with ~80 LinkedIn invitations per month
Waami AI writes outreach messages, AI prospect finder, AI list cleaner
Email outreach available on Business plan (€69/mo), including 500 email finder credits

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan — shared workspaces
featureComparison.competitor: Team features available on Enterprise plan with centralized dashboard; lower plans are individual

featureComparison.handshake: API and webhooks on all plans
featureComparison.competitor: HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce integrations + Zapier/Make with 2000+ apps
~€19/mo (~$21) for Pro plan with 300 invites/month — or free for 80 invites/month
~€345/mo (~$380) for 5 Business plans (€69 × 5) — needed for full feature access per account

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake's architecture is fundamentally different from Waalaxy's, and this difference matters enormously for safety and scalability.

First, safety. Waalaxy is primarily a Chrome extension, which is the riskiest form of LinkedIn automation. Chrome extensions inject JavaScript into LinkedIn's DOM, making them detectable. LinkedIn has gotten increasingly aggressive about flagging extension-based tools, and multiple reviews report users facing account restrictions within 90 days of using Waalaxy. Handshake is fully cloud-based with premium residential proxies included — a dramatically safer approach.

Second, scalability. Waalaxy's per-user pricing model means costs escalate quickly when managing multiple accounts. At 5 senders on Business plans, Waalaxy costs roughly $380/mo vs. Handshake's $199/mo. At 10 senders, it's $760 vs. $299. And Handshake's multi-sender rotation means your leads are distributed intelligently across accounts — Waalaxy has no rotation logic, so each account operates in isolation.

The unified inbox is another area where Handshake pulls ahead. Waalaxy's Inbox feature costs an extra €20/mo and only manages conversations for a single LinkedIn account. Handshake consolidates every reply from every sender into one view, included on all plans.

For teams and agencies, Handshake's unlimited team seats eliminate the per-user cost multiplication that makes Waalaxy expensive at scale. You pay for senders, not for the people who need access to the dashboard.
sections.competitorWins:
Waalaxy excels in accessibility and ease of use. For solo freelancers, small businesses, or anyone new to LinkedIn automation, Waalaxy's free plan and intuitive interface are hard to beat. You can get started in minutes with one of 99+ pre-built campaign templates — no technical setup required.

The pricing for individual users is very competitive. At €19/mo for 300 LinkedIn invitations per month, Waalaxy's Pro plan is one of the most affordable LinkedIn automation options available. The free plan with 80 invitations/month is genuinely useful for testing and light prospecting.

Waalaxy's AI features (Waami message writer, AI prospect finder, AI list cleaner) add genuine value for users who want AI assistance with outreach copy. Handshake doesn't offer native AI message generation.

The CRM integration ecosystem is also broader — direct integrations with HubSpot, Pipedrive, and Salesforce, plus Zapier and Make connections to 2000+ apps. For users who need tight CRM integration, Waalaxy offers more out-of-the-box options.

For individual users who want multichannel LinkedIn + email in an affordable, easy-to-use package, Waalaxy's Business plan at €69/mo is compelling.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're managing multiple LinkedIn sender accounts and account safety is a priority. It's the right choice for sales teams running multi-sender rotation, agencies managing client campaigns, and anyone who needs a cloud-based (not Chrome extension) solution with residential proxies. If you're scaling beyond 2-3 LinkedIn accounts, Handshake's pricing and architecture make it the clear winner.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Waalaxy if you're an individual user, freelancer, or small business looking for an affordable, beginner-friendly LinkedIn automation tool. The free plan is great for getting started, and the Pro plan at €19/mo is the cheapest option in the market. If you value AI-assisted message writing, pre-built templates, and don't need multi-sender rotation, Waalaxy is a solid choice — just be aware of the Chrome extension safety risks.
sections.verdict:
These tools serve fundamentally different users. Waalaxy is optimized for individual users and small teams who want simple, affordable LinkedIn automation with a low learning curve. Handshake is built for teams and agencies who need to scale outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts safely.

If you're running 1-2 accounts and want the easiest setup possible, Waalaxy's free plan or Pro tier is a great starting point. But the moment you need multi-sender rotation, a unified inbox across accounts, or enterprise-grade safety with residential proxies, Handshake is the better investment. At 5+ senders, Handshake costs half of what Waalaxy does — while offering features Waalaxy simply doesn't have.

faqs.question: Is Waalaxy a Chrome extension or cloud-based?
faqs.answer: Waalaxy is primarily a Chrome extension. It has added cloud functionality, but the core operation and login still require the Chrome extension to be installed. This makes it more detectable by LinkedIn compared to fully cloud-based tools like Handshake.

faqs.question: Is Handshake cheaper than Waalaxy?
faqs.answer: For individual users, Waalaxy is cheaper — starting at €19/mo vs. Handshake's $69/mo. But for teams managing 3+ LinkedIn accounts, Handshake becomes significantly cheaper due to its per-sender (not per-user) pricing. At 5 senders, Handshake is $199/mo vs. ~$380/mo for Waalaxy.

faqs.question: Does Waalaxy support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Each Waalaxy account operates independently. There's no built-in feature to distribute leads across multiple LinkedIn profiles or rotate senders automatically.

faqs.question: Is Waalaxy safe for my LinkedIn account?
faqs.answer: Waalaxy includes safety measures like random delays and daily limits, but its Chrome extension architecture is inherently riskier than cloud-based tools. LinkedIn can detect browser extensions that inject code into its DOM. Multiple user reports mention account restrictions after using Waalaxy. Cloud-based tools like Handshake with residential proxies are generally considered safer.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Waalaxy to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up campaigns — no data migration required. Most teams transition within a day. If you were using Waalaxy's email features, you'd need a separate cold email tool to replace that functionality.
